task_completion.description: Sketch a 100x100 mm square for the base plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A square sketch is visible in the SolveSpace viewport, ideally with 100 mm dimensions shown on the edges.

task_completion.description: Extrude the base plate to a 10 mm thickness.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The square sketch is extruded into a 3D solid plate. The property browser should show an extrusion depth of 10 mm.

task_completion.description: Sketch the first hole with a 6 mm diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circle is sketched on the face of the plate. A diameter dimension of 6 mm (or radius of 3 mm) should be visible, located in the bottom-left quadrant (coordinates -37.5, -37.5).

task_completion.description: Create a 4x4 grid pattern of the hole spaced 25 mm apart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 4x4 array of circles is visible on the plate. The property browser or constraints should indicate a step/spacing of 25 mm in both X and Y directions.

task_completion.description: Extrude the holes as a cut/difference through the plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view shows the 4x4 grid of holes passing completely through the 10 mm thick plate, revealing the background through the holes.

task_completion.description: Save the file as optical_breadboard.slvs in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar displays 'optical_breadboard.slvs', or a file manager/terminal window shows the file saved at '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/optical_breadboard.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application interface (toolbars, property browser, 3D viewport) being actively used.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the CAD software by writing the .slvs text file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are shown editing raw SolveSpace file syntax.

integrity.description: The model is a genuine 3D object in SolveSpace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The model is shown in the 3D viewport with proper shading, wireframes, or constraints typical of SolveSpace, rather than an imported 2D image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a SolveSpace model (.slvs) of an optical breadboard. Key dimensions to verify: Base plate is 100x100 mm, extruded to 10 mm depth. Holes are 6 mm in diameter, arranged in a 4x4 grid with 25 mm spacing in both X and Y directions. The first hole is centered at (-37.5, -37.5). The final file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/optical_breadboard.slvs.
